concepts.name: Vector
concepts.short_definition: An object with both magnitude and direction, often represented as an ordered list of numbers.

concepts.name: Scalar
concepts.short_definition: A quantity that has only magnitude, not direction.

concepts.name: Vector Addition
concepts.short_definition: Combining two or more vectors to produce a resultant vector.

concepts.name: Scalar Multiplication
concepts.short_definition: Multiplying a vector by a scalar, which scales its magnitude.

concepts.name: Zero Vector
concepts.short_definition: A vector with zero magnitude and undefined direction, acting as an additive identity.

concepts.name: Additive Inverse
concepts.short_definition: For a given vector, the vector that, when added to it, results in the zero vector.

concepts.name: Vector Space Axioms
concepts.short_definition: A set of ten properties that a set of vectors must satisfy to be considered a vector space.

concepts.name: Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors that is closed under vector addition and scalar multiplication, satisfying specific axioms.

concepts.name: Field
concepts.short_definition: A set of scalars with defined addition and multiplication operations that satisfy certain properties.

concepts.name: Real Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space where the scalars are real numbers.

concepts.name: Complex Vector Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space where the scalars are complex numbers.

concepts.name: Subspace
concepts.short_definition: A subset of a vector space that is itself a vector space under the same operations.

concepts.name: Span
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible linear combinations of a given set of vectors.

concepts.name: Linear Combination
concepts.short_definition: A sum of vectors, each multiplied by a scalar.

concepts.name: Linearly Independent
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where the only linear combination that equals the zero vector is the trivial one (all scalars are zero).

concepts.name: Linearly Dependent
concepts.short_definition: A set of vectors where a non-trivial linear combination equals the zero vector.

concepts.name: Basis
concepts.short_definition: A set of linearly independent vectors that span the entire vector space.

concepts.name: Standard Basis
concepts.short_definition: A specific, commonly used basis for R^n, consisting of vectors with a single 1 and the rest 0s.

concepts.name: Dimension
concepts.short_definition: The number of vectors in any basis for a vector space.

concepts.name: Finite-Dimensional
concepts.short_definition: A vector space with a finite dimension.

concepts.name: Infinite-Dimensional
concepts.short_definition: A vector space with an infinite dimension.

concepts.name: Coordinate Vector
concepts.short_definition: The representation of a vector as a list of scalars with respect to a chosen basis.

concepts.name: Isomorphism
concepts.short_definition: A linear transformation between two vector spaces that is bijective, meaning they are structurally identical.

concepts.name: Direct Sum
concepts.short_definition: A way to construct a new vector space from two or more vector spaces.

concepts.name: Quotient Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space formed by taking a vector space and dividing it by a subspace.

concepts.name: Polynomial Space
concepts.short_definition: The vector space of all polynomials of degree at most n.

concepts.name: Function Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space where the elements are functions.

concepts.name: Matrix Space
concepts.short_definition: The vector space of all m x n matrices.

concepts.name: Inner Product Space
concepts.short_definition: A vector space equipped with an inner product operation.

concepts.name: Inner Product
concepts.short_definition: A function that takes two vectors and returns a scalar, satisfying certain properties.

concepts.name: Norm
concepts.short_definition: A function that assigns a length or magnitude to each vector in a vector space.

concepts.name: Orthogonality
concepts.short_definition: The property of two vectors being perpendicular, often indicated by an inner product of zero.

concepts.name: Orthonormal Basis
concepts.short_definition: A basis where all basis vectors are mutually orthogonal and have a norm of 1.

concepts.name: Gram-Schmidt Process
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm for constructing an orthonormal basis from any given basis.

concepts.name: Linear Transformation
concepts.short_definition: A function between two vector spaces that preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication.

concepts.name: Kernel
concepts.short_definition: The set of all vectors in the domain of a linear transformation that map to the zero vector in the codomain.

concepts.name: Image
concepts.short_definition: The set of all vectors in the codomain of a linear transformation that are the result of applying the transformation to some vector in the domain.

concepts.name: Rank-Nullity Theorem
concepts.short_definition: A theorem relating the dimension of the kernel (nullity) and the dimension of the image (rank) of a linear transformation.

concepts.name: Dual Space
concepts.short_definition: The vector space of all linear functionals on a given vector space.
